---
title: "About"
---

# About Current Findings

Current Findings are short, open scientific documents from Roberts Lab and collaborators. They may include preliminary analyses, technical notes, field reports, exploratory datasets, teaching-adjacent analyses, negative results, and small manuscripts that are useful to share but not necessarily suited for formal peer-reviewed publication.

Reports are not peer reviewed unless explicitly stated. They should be interpreted as working scientific communications. We welcome corrections, comments, reuse, and collaboration.

## Report types

Reports may be labeled as:

- Working manuscript
- Technical note
- Dataset note
- Field report
- Methods note
- Negative result
- Student research note
- Exploratory analysis

## Suggested use

These reports are intended to support open science, transparency, early sharing, student training, and reuse of small scientific efforts that might otherwise remain hidden.

## Review status

By default, reports on this site are not peer reviewed. Authors may revise reports over time. Readers should check the report date, version history, and data/code availability sections before reusing or citing a report.

## Corrections and comments

Corrections, comments, and suggestions are welcome through GitHub issues or direct correspondence with the report authors.
